allowed_resolution_classes.summary: Use only if the evaluator is asserting the wrong bundle identifier, amendment family, or change-receipt family.
Use when the active bundle hash is correct but one or more required support artifacts are absent from the active tree.
Use when the active tree has advanced to a new bundle hash and needs a new lawful amendment/change-receipt chain that supersedes the prior documented hash.
Use when a stale pin or historical support surface is still being treated like current truth and must be explicitly demoted to lineage-only.

claim_boundary: This packet freezes tranche T01 only. It does not change package truth, truth-engine law, replay timing on main, or archive/canonical authority boundaries.
do_not_widen_boundaries: Do not change truth-engine derivation law.
Do not rerun the main truth-engine replay early.
Do not let prep lanes become authoritative.
Do not widen Track 03 or package promotion to chase green checks.

live_resolution_read.recommended_resolution_summary: The active tree has advanced to a new bundle hash, while the current support chain still terminates at the prior pinned hash.

precedence_rule.resolution_decision_rule: Prefer binding a new lawful amendment/change-receipt chain for the active hash over weakening the evaluator contract.
precedence_rule.staleness_decision_rule: If the current active-tree hash lacks a matching kt.law_amendment.v2 or kt.law_bundle_change_receipt.v1 while the pinned hash still has them, the support chain is stale relative to the active tree; the evaluator contract is not stale.
precedence_rule.winning_rule: The active-tree LAW_BUNDLE hash computed from LAW_BUNDLE_FL3.json membership and current member bytes outranks the pinned sha file and prior amendment/change-receipt lineage when they disagree.
